name: list_library_models
description: Returns published library listings — public listings plus any explicitly shared with the caller's organization.

name: get_library_model
description: Gets a library model by listing identity

name: get_model
description: Gets a model by ID

name: list_models
description: Gets all models

name: delete_model
description: Deletes a model by ID

name: deploy_model_from_library
description: Creates a new model in the caller's organization. The `source` field selects how the model is constructed (currently: `library_listing` — fork an accessible listing from `GET /v1/library_models`). The deployment isn't instantly ready — poll GET endpoint until status is ACTIVE.

CreateModelRequestV1.description: Body for creating a model via `POST /v1/models`.

source.description: Where the new model is created from.
source.title: Source

CreateModelRequestV1.title: CreateModelRequestV1

DeploymentArchivePayloadV1.description:
Deployment-level fields for a model-archive push.

Shared by every endpoint that creates a deployment from an uploaded archive:
`POST /v1/prepare_model_upload`, the `model_archive` source on `POST
/v1/models`, and `POST /v1/models/{model_id}/deployments`.

config.description: Parsed model config as a JSON object.
config.title: Config

raw_config.description: Original config.yaml text, persisted as-is on the deployment. Best-effort: invalid raw configs are logged and dropped without failing the request.
raw_config.title: Raw Config

user_env.description: Client environment metadata (e.g. client version, Python version). Validated server-side.
user_env.title: User Env

environment_name.description: Stable environment to push to (e.g. `production`). If unset, the deployment is created without environment selection. Caller must have push permission for the named environment.
environment_name.title: Environment Name

preserve_env_instance_type.description: Retain the target environment's current instance type rather than the one in `config`. Only meaningful when `environment_name` is set and that environment already exists.
preserve_env_instance_type.title: Preserve Env Instance Type

deploy_timeout_minutes.description: Deploy timeout in minutes; allowed range 10 to 1440. Server default applies if unset.
deploy_timeout_minutes.title: Deploy Timeout Minutes

deployment_name.description: Optional human-readable name for the deployment.
deployment_name.title: Deployment Name

labels.description: User-provided key-value labels for the deployment.
labels.title: Labels

is_development.description: If true, push as a development deployment: the model's single mutable dev slot, created if absent and overwritten in place otherwise. The following fields must be left at their defaults: `environment_name`, `preserve_env_instance_type`, `deployment_name`.
is_development.title: Is Development

DeploymentArchivePayloadV1.title: DeploymentArchivePayloadV1

LibraryListingSourceV1.description: Create a model by forking a library listing accessible to the caller's organization.

lab_display_name.description: Identifier of the publishing organization, as returned by `GET /v1/library_models`.
lab_display_name.title: Lab Display Name

user_defined_listing_id.description: Listing identifier within the publishing organization.
user_defined_listing_id.title: User Defined Listing Id

deployed_model_name.description: Optional name for the new deployed model. Defaults to the listing's configured name.
deployed_model_name.title: Deployed Model Name

LibraryListingSourceV1.title: LibraryListingSourceV1

ModelArchiveSourceV1.description:
Create a model from an archive previously uploaded via the credentials
issued by `POST /v1/prepare_model_upload`.

deployment.description: Deployment-level configuration for the model's first deployment.

s3_key.description: S3 key of the uploaded archive, from the credentials returned by `POST /v1/prepare_model_upload`.
s3_key.title: S3 Key

disable_archive_download.description: If true, the uploaded archive is not downloadable after creation. Locked at model creation; cannot be changed by subsequent deployments.
disable_archive_download.title: Disable Archive Download

ModelArchiveSourceV1.title: ModelArchiveSourceV1

name: list_model_apis
description: List Model APIs visible to the caller. By default returns the full catalog; pass `added_only=true` to restrict to Model APIs the workspace has added.

cursor.description: Opaque cursor returned by a previous page. Omit to fetch the first page.
cursor.title: Cursor

limit.description: Maximum number of items to return.
limit.title: Limit

added_only.description: When true, restrict the result to Model APIs the workspace has added. Defaults to the full visible catalog.
added_only.title: Added Only

name: get_model_api
description: Fetch a Model API by name, with workspace overlay when added.

name: list_instance_types
description: Gets all available instance types

name: list_instance_type_prices
description: Gets prices for available instance types.

name: get_deployment
description: Gets a model's deployment by ID and returns the deployment.

name: list_deployments_for_model
description: Gets all deployments of a model

name: delete_deployment
description: Deletes a model's deployment by ID and returns the tombstone of the deployment.

name: get_deployment_download_url
description: Gets a presigned URL to download the truss tar file for a deployment.

name: get_deployment_config
description: Returns the deployment's config. `output_format` query param picks the shape: 'raw' (config.yaml text), 'parsed' (dict with defaults), or 'both' (default).

name: get_deployment_logs
description: Gets all the logs for a model deployment in the given time range, which defaults to the last 30 minutes. A failed or older deployment may only have logs from before that window; pass `start_epoch_millis` to widen it back to the build/deploy time.

GetDeploymentLogsRequestV1.description: A request to fetch deployment logs.

start_epoch_millis.description: Epoch milliseconds at which to start fetching logs. Defaults to 30 minutes before the end. The window from start to end must not exceed 7 days.
start_epoch_millis.title: Start Epoch Millis

end_epoch_millis.description: Epoch milliseconds at which to stop fetching logs. Defaults to the current time.
end_epoch_millis.title: End Epoch Millis

limit.description: Limit of logs to fetch in a single request
limit.title: Limit

min_level.description: Minimum log severity to include. Omit to return all log lines, including lines that have no level. Any explicit value returns lines at or above that severity and drops lines without a level.

replica.description: Only return logs emitted by this replica (5-char short ID).
replica.title: Replica

request_id.description: Only return logs tagged with this inference request ID.
request_id.title: Request Id

component.description: Only return logs from this component.
component.title: Component

search_pattern.description: RE2 regular expression matched against the log message. Prefer `includes` and `excludes` for plain substring matches.
search_pattern.title: Search Pattern

includes.description: Case-sensitive substrings that must all appear in the log message.
includes.title: Includes

excludes.description: Case-sensitive substrings; lines containing any of these are dropped.
excludes.title: Excludes

GetDeploymentLogsRequestV1.title: GetDeploymentLogsRequestV1

name: get_deployment_metrics
description: Gets the metrics for a model deployment in the given time range.

DeploymentMetricModeV1.description: How metric values are aggregated over the request.
DeploymentMetricModeV1.title: DeploymentMetricModeV1

mode.description: 'CURRENT': a single instantaneous snapshot at now; start/end must be omitted. 'SUMMARY': a single value set aggregating the whole window. 'SERIES': evenly-spaced value sets across the window, with the step derived from the window duration.

start_epoch_millis.description: Epoch millis timestamp to start fetching metrics. Defaults to one hour before the end.
start_epoch_millis.title: Start Epoch Millis

end_epoch_millis.description: Epoch millis timestamp to end fetching metrics. Defaults to the current time. The window between start and end must not exceed 7 days.
end_epoch_millis.title: End Epoch Millis

metrics.description: Names of the metrics to return; see https://docs.baseten.co/observability/export-metrics/supported-metrics for the available names. When omitted, a default set is returned: baseten_replicas_active, baseten_inference_requests_total, and baseten_end_to_end_response_time_seconds. Unknown names are rejected; valid names that do not apply to the deployment are omitted from the response.
metrics.title: Metrics

name: create_deployment_patch
description: Persists a patch durably without applying it; call the sync endpoint to apply staged patches to the running deployment. The target must be a development deployment (its archive created with `is_development` set to `true`); patching any other deployment is rejected.

CreateDeploymentPatchRequestV1.description:
A patch to stage against the development deployment.

Staging is durable on its own: the patch is persisted independently of the
later sync, so a failed sync does not lose it.

prev_patch_hash.description: Content hash of the patch point this patch is applied on - the link the staged patch must build on. A stale value (the base moved underneath the client) is rejected with a conflict.
prev_patch_hash.title: Prev Patch Hash

next_patch_point.description: The source state after this patch. The server derives its content hash from `content_hashes`.

patch_ops.description: The ordered ops that make up this patch. At least one op is required; a patch that changes nothing is not a valid request. There is no op for a directory: a directory comes into existence when the first file under it is added, and is removed when its last file is removed, so directory creation and deletion happen implicitly through the file ops. Adding or removing an otherwise empty directory therefore produces no ops even though it changes the source hash; do not send a patch request for such a change.
patch_ops.title: Patch Ops

CreateDeploymentPatchRequestV1.title: CreateDeploymentPatchRequestV1

DeploymentPatchActionV1.description: How a patch op changes its target.
DeploymentPatchActionV1.title: DeploymentPatchActionV1

DeploymentPatchOpConfigV1.description:
Replace the config when config.yaml changes.

Config has no action: it is always a full replacement of the parsed config.
Derived changes (environment variables, external data, requirements) are
emitted as their own ops alongside this one.

config.description: The full parsed config as a JSON object.
config.title: Config

path.description: Config file path within the source.
path.title: Path

DeploymentPatchOpConfigV1.title: DeploymentPatchOpConfigV1

DeploymentPatchOpEnvVarV1.description: Add, update, or remove a single environment variable.

action.description: How this op changes the variable.

value.description: The environment variable value. Required for add and update.
value.title: Value

DeploymentPatchOpEnvVarV1.title: DeploymentPatchOpEnvVarV1

DeploymentPatchOpExternalDataV1.description:
Add, update, or remove a single external data item.

External data is referenced by config, not stored in the source. The backend
only adds or removes it, where adding re-downloads (overwriting any existing
file), so `update` is accepted and treated identically to `add`.

action.description: How this op changes the item. `UPDATE` is treated identically to `ADD`.

item.description: The single external data item descriptor.
item.title: Item

DeploymentPatchOpExternalDataV1.title: DeploymentPatchOpExternalDataV1

DeploymentPatchOpModelCodeV1.description: Add, update, or remove a file under the model code directory.

action.description: How this op changes the file.

path.description: File path relative to the model code directory.
path.title: Path

content.description: UTF-8 file content. Null for removals and binary files.
content.title: Content

hot_reload.description: Whether the running server can pick up this change without a restart.
hot_reload.title: Hot Reload

DeploymentPatchOpModelCodeV1.title: DeploymentPatchOpModelCodeV1

DeploymentPatchOpPackageV1.description: Add, update, or remove a file under the bundled packages directory.

action.description: How this op changes the file.

path.description: File path relative to the bundled packages directory.
path.title: Path

content.description: UTF-8 file content. Null for removals and binary files.
content.title: Content

DeploymentPatchOpPackageV1.title: DeploymentPatchOpPackageV1

DeploymentPatchOpPythonRequirementV1.description: Add, update, or remove a single Python requirement.

action.description: How this op changes the requirement.

requirement.description: The requirement to apply. For removals this is the package name; otherwise the full requirements.txt-style line.
requirement.title: Requirement

DeploymentPatchOpPythonRequirementV1.title: DeploymentPatchOpPythonRequirementV1

DeploymentPatchPointV1.description:
A patch point: the source state the next patch is computed against.

The content hash that identifies a point is derived from this state (see
`DeploymentPatchPointWithHashV1.hash`), so a request only sends the state and
the server stamps the hash. A previous point's hash plus the current local
source is enough to compute the next patch, so the watch client reads the
point it is patching off of.

content_hashes.description: Map of every non-ignored source path, relative and forward-slash, to its content hash: files map to the hex blake3 digest of their bytes, directories map to null. This is the full signature of the source tree and what the content hash is derived from.
content_hashes.title: Content Hashes

config.description: The verbatim config.yaml text for this source state.
config.title: Config

requirements.description: Requirements resolved from the config's requirements file, when it points at one. Empty when requirements are declared inline in the config.
requirements.title: Requirements

DeploymentPatchPointV1.title: DeploymentPatchPointV1

name: get_deployment_patches_state
description: Returns the patch point the deployment is recorded as running and the latest staged-but-unsynced point, if any. The watch client computes its next patch off the pending point when present, else the running point.

name: sync_deployment_patches
description: Applies any staged patches to the running deployment. A 2xx response means the sync reached a verdict: in sync, or a full push is required (needs_full_deploy_reason). A 503 means the sync was not attempted or failed recoverably and should be retried; any other error is terminal.

SyncDeploymentPatchesRequestV1.description:
Triggers a sync of any staged patches to the running deployment. Takes no
fields: the deployment and its staged patches fully determine the sync.
SyncDeploymentPatchesRequestV1.title: SyncDeploymentPatchesRequestV1

Returns logs from every deployment that was active on the environment over
the requested time range, merged in timestamp order.

min_level.description: Minimum log severity to include. Omit to return all log lines, including lines that have no level. Any explicit value returns lines at or above that severity and drops lines without a level.

replica.description: Only return logs emitted by this replica (5-char short ID).
replica.title: Replica

request_id.description: Only return logs tagged with this inference request ID.

component.description: Only return logs from this component.

search_pattern.description: RE2 regular expression matched against the log message. Prefer `includes` and `excludes` for plain substring matches.

name: update_environment
description: Asynchronously updates an environment's settings. Poll the GET endpoint for the applied state.

UpdateAutoscalingSettingsV1.description: A request to update autoscaling settings for a deployment. All fields are optional, and we only update ones passed in.

autoscaling_window.description: Timeframe of traffic considered for autoscaling decisions
autoscaling_window.title: Autoscaling Window

scale_down_delay.description: Waiting period before scaling down any active replica
scale_down_delay.title: Scale Down Delay

concurrency_target.description: Number of requests per replica before scaling up
concurrency_target.title: Concurrency Target

target_utilization_percentage.description: Target utilization percentage for scaling up/down.
target_utilization_percentage.title: Target Utilization Percentage

target_in_flight_tokens.description: Target number of in-flight tokens for autoscaling decisions. Early access only.
target_in_flight_tokens.title: Target In Flight Tokens

max_scale_down_rate.description: Maximum percentage of replicas that can be removed per autoscaling window (1–50). E.g. 20 means at most 20% of replicas are removed per window.
max_scale_down_rate.title: Max Scale Down Rate

UpdateAutoscalingSettingsV1.title: UpdateAutoscalingSettingsV1

UpdateEnvironmentRequestV1.description: A request to update an environment.
UpdateEnvironmentRequestV1.title: UpdateEnvironmentRequestV1

redeploy_on_promotion.description: Whether to deploy on all promotions. Enabling this flag allows model code to safely handle environment-specific logic. When a deployment is promoted, a new deployment will be created with a copy of the image.
redeploy_on_promotion.title: Redeploy On Promotion

rolling_deploy.description: Whether the environment should rely on rolling deploy orchestration.
rolling_deploy.title: Rolling Deploy

promotion_cleanup_strategy.description: The cleanup strategy to use after a promotion completes.

ramp_up_while_promoting.description: Whether to ramp up traffic while promoting
ramp_up_while_promoting.title: Ramp Up While Promoting

ramp_up_duration_seconds.description: Duration of the ramp up in seconds
ramp_up_duration_seconds.title: Ramp Up Duration Seconds

UpdateRollingDeployConfigV1.description: Rolling deploy config for promoting chains and oracles

rolling_deploy_strategy.description: The rolling deploy strategy to use for promotions.

max_surge_percent.description: The maximum surge percentage for rolling deploys.
max_surge_percent.title: Max Surge Percent

max_unavailable_percent.description: The maximum unavailable percentage for rolling deploys.
max_unavailable_percent.title: Max Unavailable Percent

stabilization_time_seconds.description: The stabilization time in seconds for rolling deploys.
stabilization_time_seconds.title: Stabilization Time Seconds

replica_overhead_percent.description: The replica overhead percentage for rolling deploys.
replica_overhead_percent.title: Replica Overhead Percent

name: activate_environment
description: Activates an inactive deployment associated with an environment and returns the activation status.

name: deactivate_environment
description: Deactivates a deployment associated with an environment and returns the deactivation status.

name: promote_to_environment
description: Promotes an existing deployment to an environment and returns the promoted deployment.

PromoteToEnvironmentRequestV1.description: A request to promote a deployment to a environment.

scale_down_previous_deployment.description: Whether to scale down the previous deployment after promoting
scale_down_previous_deployment.title: Scale Down Previous Deployment

deployment_id.description: The id of the deployment to promote
deployment_id.title: Deployment Id

preserve_env_instance_type.description: Whether to use the promoting deployment's instance type or preserve target environment's instance type
preserve_env_instance_type.title: Preserve Env Instance Type

PromoteToEnvironmentRequestV1.title: PromoteToEnvironmentRequestV1

name: cancel_promotion
description: Cancels an ongoing promotion to an environment and returns the cancellation status.

name: pause_promotion
description: Pauses an in-progress rolling promotion after the current step completes. No further scaling changes are made until resumed.

name: resume_promotion
description: Resumes a paused rolling promotion, continuing from where it was paused.

name: force_cancel_promotion
description: Immediately cancels an in-progress rolling promotion and triggers rollback to the previous version.

name: force_roll_forward_promotion
description: Immediately completes the rolling promotion, shifting all traffic to the new version. This works even if the promotion is in the process of rolling back.

UpdateChainletEnvironmentAutoscalingSett.description:
A request to update the autoscaling settings for a multiple chainlets in an environment.
If a chainlet name doesn't exist, an error is returned.

updates.description: Mapping of chainlet name to the desired chainlet autoscaling settings. If the chainlet name doesn't exist, an error is returned.
updates.title: Updates

UpdateChainletEnvironmentAutoscalingSett.title: UpdateChainletEnvironmentAutoscalingSettingsRequestV1

name: update_chainlet_environment_instance_type
description: Updates a chainlet environment's instance type settings. The chainlet environment setting must exist. When updated, a new chain deployment is created and deployed. It is promoted to the chain environment according to promotion settings on the environment.

ChainletEnvironmentInstanceTypeUpdateV1.description: A request to update the environment settings for a chainlet.

instance_type_id.description: Key of the instance type to use for the chainlet
instance_type_id.title: Instance Type Id

ChainletEnvironmentInstanceTypeUpdateV1.title: ChainletEnvironmentInstanceTypeUpdateV1

UpdateChainletEnvironmentInstanceTypeReq.description:
A request to update the instance types for chainlets in an environment. Multiples
updates can be made in one request. The updates will be processed in batch and a new deployment
will be created, deployed and promoted into the environment.

updates.description: Mapping of chainlet name to the desired chainlet instance type. If the chainlet name doesn't exist, an error is returned.
updates.title: Updates

UpdateChainletEnvironmentInstanceTypeReq.title: UpdateChainletEnvironmentInstanceTypeRequestV1

name: list_secrets
description: Gets all secrets (metadata only, no plain text keys)

name: list_team_secrets
description: Gets all secrets for a team (metadata only, no plain text keys)

name: upsert_secret
description: Creates or updates a secret by name. Scoped to the caller's primary team — use the team-scoped variant to target a specific team.

UpsertSecretRequestV1.description: A request to create or update a Baseten secret by name.

name.description: Name of the new or existing secret
name.title: Name

UpsertSecretRequestV1.title: UpsertSecretRequestV1

name: upsert_team_secret
description: Creates a new secret or updates an existing secret if one with the provided name already exists. The name and creation date of the created or updated secret is returned. This secret belongs to the specified team

UpsertSecretRequestV1.description: A request to create or update a Baseten secret by name.

name.description: Name of the new or existing secret
name.title: Name

UpsertSecretRequestV1.title: UpsertSecretRequestV1

name: delete_secret
description: Deletes a secret by name in the specified team, or in the caller's organization default team when no team is specified.

name: list_teams
description: Returns a list of all teams the authenticated user has access to.

name: get_team
description: Returns a team the authenticated user has access to.

name: list_environment_groups
description: Lists the environment groups for the team specified in the path, or for the caller's organization default team when no team is specified.

name: get_environment_group
description: Gets a single environment group by name on the team specified in the path, or on the caller's organization default team when no team is specified.

name: update_environment_group
description: Sets whether the environment is restricted and replaces the list of users granted manage access. Targets the team specified in the path, or the caller's organization default team when no team is specified.

UpdateEnvironmentGroupManageAccessV1.description: Manage-access settings to apply to an environment group.

is_restricted.description: Whether to restrict this environment to a specific set of users.
is_restricted.title: Is Restricted

user_ids.description: IDs of users granted manage access while restricted. Only meaningful when is_restricted is true.
user_ids.title: User Ids

UpdateEnvironmentGroupManageAccessV1.title: UpdateEnvironmentGroupManageAccessV1

UpdateEnvironmentGroupRequestV1.description: A request to update an existing environment group.

manage_access.description: Manage-access settings to apply. Omit to leave manage access unchanged.

name: list_api_keys
description: Lists the user's API keys (metadata only, no plain text keys)

name: delete_api_key
description: Deletes an API key by prefix and returns info about the API key.

name: get_billing_usage_summary
description: Returns billing usage data within the specified date range. Includes dedicated model serving, training, and model APIs usage. The date range must not exceed 31 days.

end_date.description: End date in ISO 8601 format (UTC). Date range cannot exceed 31 days.
end_date.title: End Date

name: list_training_projects
description: List all training projects for the organization.

name: get_training_project
description: Get the details of an existing training project.

name: upsert_training_project
description: Upserts a training project with the specified metadata.

UpsertTrainingProjectRequestV1.description: A request to upsert a training project.
UpsertTrainingProjectRequestV1.title: UpsertTrainingProjectRequestV1

UpsertTrainingProjectV1.description: Fields that can be upserted on a training project.
UpsertTrainingProjectV1.title: UpsertTrainingProjectV1

name: delete_training_project
description: Deletes a training project and all associated training jobs.

name: create_training_job
description: Creates a training job with the specified configuration.

access_key_secret_ref.description: Name of the access key secret

secret_access_key_secret_ref.description: Name of the secret key secret

project_name.description: Name of the project to load the checkpoint from
project_name.title: Project Name

job_id.description: ID of the job to load the checkpoint from
job_id.title: Job Id

checkpoint_name.description: Name of the checkpoint to load from
checkpoint_name.title: Checkpoint Name

project_name.description: Name of the project to load the checkpoint from
project_name.title: Project Name

job_id.description: ID of the job to load the checkpoint from
job_id.title: Job Id

CreateJobWeightConfigV1.description:
Weight source configuration for MDN (Model Distribution Network).

Enables training jobs to mount external model weights from HuggingFace, S3, GCS, or R2
via MDN's caching and CSI mounting infrastructure. Weights are mirrored once and
deduplicated across training jobs.

source.description: Weight source URI. Supported formats: hf://, s3://, gs://, r2://
source.title: Source

mount_location.description: Path where weights will be mounted in the container
mount_location.title: Mount Location

allow_patterns.description: File patterns to include (Unix-style shell patterns)
allow_patterns.title: Allow Patterns

ignore_patterns.description: File patterns to exclude (Unix-style shell patterns)
ignore_patterns.title: Ignore Patterns

auth_secret_name.description: Name of the workspace secret for authentication (e.g., HuggingFace token)
auth_secret_name.title: Auth Secret Name

auth.description: Authentication configuration for the weight source.
auth.title: Auth

CreateJobWeightConfigV1.title: CreateJobWeightConfigV1

accelerator.description: GPU type for the training job.
accelerator.title: Accelerator

count.description: GPUs needed for the training job.
count.title: Count

enable_legacy_hf_mount.description: Whether to enable the legacy Hugging Face cache.
enable_legacy_hf_mount.title: Enable Legacy Hf Mount

enabled.description: Whether to enable the read-write cache.
enabled.title: Enabled

require_cache_affinity.description: Whether to require region affinity for the read-write cache. If False, the resulting job is not guaranteed to be deployed alongside the previous cache.
require_cache_affinity.title: Require Cache Affinity

mount_base_path.description: Mount base path for the cache directory. The project cache and team cache will be mounted under this path.
mount_base_path.title: Mount Base Path

checkpoint_path.description: path where checkpoints will be saved.
checkpoint_path.title: Checkpoint Path

volume_size_gib.description: Size of the volume in gibibytes. If not provided, the default size will be used
volume_size_gib.title: Volume Size Gib

CreateTrainingJobComputeV1.description: Configuration to specify the compute for a training job.

node_count.description: Number of nodes for the training job.
node_count.title: Node Count

cpu_count.description: Number of cpus for the training job.
cpu_count.title: Cpu Count

accelerator.description: GPU specification for the training job

availability_model.description: Capacity guarantee for the job. 'dedicated' (the default) runs on on-demand capacity that is not preempted. 'spot' runs on interruptible capacity that may be preempted; the user is responsible for checkpointing their own progress.

CreateTrainingJobComputeV1.title: CreateTrainingJobComputeV1

CreateTrainingJobImageV1.description: Configuration to create a training job image.

base_image.description: Base image for the training job.
base_image.title: Base Image

CreateTrainingJobImageV1.title: CreateTrainingJobImageV1

CreateTrainingJobRequestV1.description: A request to create a training job.
CreateTrainingJobRequestV1.title: CreateTrainingJobRequestV1

CreateTrainingJobRuntimeV1.description: Configuration to specify the runtime environment for a training job.

start_commands.description: Commands to execute when starting the runtime.
start_commands.title: Start Commands

environment_variables.description: Environment variables to set in the runtime.
environment_variables.title: Environment Variables

artifacts.description: Runtime artifacts for the training job.
artifacts.title: Artifacts

CreateTrainingJobRuntimeV1.title: CreateTrainingJobRuntimeV1

s3_bucket.description: S3 bucket for the uploaded runtime artifact.
s3_bucket.title: S3 Bucket

s3_key.description: S3 key for the uploaded runtime artifact.
s3_key.title: S3 Key

runtime.description: Configuration for the runtime environment of the training job.

weights.description: MDN weight sources to mount in the training container. Weights are mirrored and cached for fast startup.
weights.title: Weights

enable_baseten_workdir.description: When enabled, uses /b10/workspace as the working directory instead of the image WORKDIR.
enable_baseten_workdir.title: Enable Baseten Workdir

priority.description: Queue priority. Higher values are dequeued first. Defaults to 0.
priority.title: Priority

auth_method.description: Method to authenticate with the registry

gcp_service_account_json_docker_auth.description: GCP service account details for the registry

aws_oidc_docker_auth.description: AWS OIDC details for the registry

gcp_oidc_docker_auth.description: GCP OIDC details for the registry

registry_secret_docker_auth.description: Required when auth_method is REGISTRY_SECRET. Supports any Docker registry (Docker Hub, GHCR, NGC, etc.) via username:password credentials stored as a Baseten secret.

GcpOidcDockerAuthV1.description: GCP OIDC details for the registry.

service_account.description: GCP service account name for OIDC authentication
service_account.title: Service Account

workload_identity_provider.description: GCP workload identity provider for OIDC authentication
workload_identity_provider.title: Workload Identity Provider

GcpOidcDockerAuthV1.title: GcpOidcDockerAuthV1

service_account_json_secret_ref.description: Name of the service account secret

InteractiveSessionConfigV1.description: Configuration for interactive debugging sessions on training jobs.

trigger.description: When to create the interactive session. 'on_startup' creates on job start, 'on_failure' creates on job failure, 'on_demand' bypasses automatic session creation.

timeout_minutes.description: Number of minutes before the interactive session times out.
timeout_minutes.title: Timeout Minutes

session_provider.description: The IDE client for the interactive session.

auth_provider.description: The authentication provider for the interactive session.

InteractiveSessionConfigV1.title: InteractiveSessionConfigV1

download_folder.description: Folder where checkpoints will be downloaded
download_folder.title: Download Folder

run_id.description: ID of the Loops run to load the checkpoint from
run_id.title: Run Id

checkpoint_name.description: Name of the checkpoint to load
checkpoint_name.title: Checkpoint Name

target.description: Which checkpoint target to load: 'trainer' (full training state) or 'sampler' (inference weights)
target.title: Target

RegistrySecretDockerAuthV1.description:
Authentication via a Baseten secret for any Docker registry (Docker Hub, GHCR, NGC, etc.).
The referenced secret must contain credentials in the format 'username:password'.
For Docker Hub, set registry to 'https://index.docker.io/v1/'. For GHCR, use 'ghcr.io'.

secret_ref.description: Reference to a Baseten secret containing credentials in the format 'username:password'

RegistrySecretDockerAuthV1.title: RegistrySecretDockerAuthV1

name.description: Name of the secret to reference.
name.title: Name

TrussUserEnv.description:
This data models is used to flexibly store info alongside oracle versions.

There is a corresponding data model in the truss client.
In contrast, here all fields are optional for backwards compatibility with old
clients.
TrussUserEnv.title: TrussUserEnv

V1AvailabilityModel.description:
Capacity guarantee under which a training job is scheduled.

``DEDICATED`` is on-demand capacity that is not preempted (the default). ``SPOT`` is
interruptible capacity that may be preempted; the user is responsible for checkpointing
their own progress. A managed/resumable model where the platform handles
checkpoint/resume on its own is intentionally not defined yet; it is planned for a
future milestone.
V1AvailabilityModel.title: V1AvailabilityModel

name: list_training_jobs_for_project
description: List all training jobs for the training project.

name: get_training_job
description: Get the details of an existing training job.

name: update_training_job_priority
description: Update mutable fields on a training job. Currently supports changing the queue priority of a PENDING job; higher priorities are dequeued first.

UpdateTrainingJobRequestV1.description: A request to update mutable fields on a training job.

priority.description: New queue priority for a PENDING training job. Higher values are dequeued first. Only jobs in the PENDING state can have their priority changed.
priority.title: Priority

UpdateTrainingJobRequestV1.title: UpdateTrainingJobRequestV1

name: delete_training_job
description: Deletes a training job. Stops it first if still running.

name: stop_training_job
description: Stops a training job.

StopTrainingJobRequestV1.description: A request to stop a training job.
StopTrainingJobRequestV1.title: StopTrainingJobRequestV1

name: recreate_training_job
description: Create a new training job with the same configuration as an existing training job.

name: get_training_job_checkpoints
description: Get the checkpoints for a training job.

name: get_training_job_checkpoint_files
description: Get presigned URLs for all checkpoint files for a training job.

name: download_training_job_artifacts
description: Get the uploaded training job as a S3 Artifact

name: get_training_job_auth_codes
description: Get authentication codes for all nodes of a training job's interactive sessions.

name: patch_interactive_session
description: Update specific fields on a training job's interactive session. Only provided (non-null) fields are updated.

PatchInteractiveSessionRequestV1.description:
Request to patch an interactive session.

Only fields that are provided (non-None) will be applied.

timeout_minutes.description: For on_startup sessions, minutes to add to the expiration. For on_demand/on_failure sessions, minutes to add to the timeout. Use -1 for infinite timeout (bumps by 10 years).
timeout_minutes.title: Timeout Minutes

trigger.description: Update when the interactive session is created. Cannot be changed if the session trigger is 'on_startup'.

PatchInteractiveSessionRequestV1.title: PatchInteractiveSessionRequestV1

name: sign_ssh_certificate_training_job
description: Signs a short-lived SSH certificate granting access to a specific training job pod. Returns the signed SSH certificate, a JWT token for SSH proxy authentication, the proxy address to connect through, and the certificate expiry time.

SignSSHCertificateRequestV1.description: Request to sign an SSH certificate for accessing a workload pod.

public_key.description: The user's SSH public key (e.g., 'ssh-ed25519 AAAA... user@host').
public_key.title: Public Key

replica_id.description: The replica to connect to. Required for training jobs (e.g. '0'). Optional for inference (server picks a running replica if omitted).
replica_id.title: Replica Id

SignSSHCertificateRequestV1.title: SignSSHCertificateRequestV1

name: get_training_project_cache_summary
description: Get the cache summary for the most recent training job in the project.

name: get_training_gpu_capacity
description: Returns GPU capacity limits (baseline and peak) and current usage for the organization.

name: update_team_training_gpu_capacity
description: Sets the max concurrent GPUs of a given type a team may use. Creates the limit if one doesn't exist for this (team, gpu_type) pair, otherwise updates it in place. Org-admin only: this is a ceiling the org imposes on the team, not something the team manages for itself.

PatchTeamTrainingGpuCapacityRequestV1.description:
A request to set the GPU capacity ceiling for a (team, gpu_type) pair.

Creates the limit if one doesn't already exist for this team and GPU type,
otherwise updates it in place.

max_gpus.description: Max concurrent GPUs of this type the team may use
max_gpus.title: Max Gpus

PatchTeamTrainingGpuCapacityRequestV1.title: PatchTeamTrainingGpuCapacityRequestV1

name: get_training_job_queue_context
description: Returns the (org, gpu_type) capacity pool the job was gated by, jobs that were holding GPU capacity in that pool when this job was submitted, and every TrainingJobStatus event in [submitted_at, released_at] for those jobs — useful for understanding why a job sat in PENDING. Caller must be an org admin and the job must belong to the caller's org.

name: get_user_training_workload_plane_overrides
description: Returns all per-user training workload-plane override entries for the user identified by ID in the URL path.

name: update_user_training_workload_plane_overrides
description: Applies per-entry changes to the user's training workload-plane overrides. Each item upserts the (gpu_type, is_multinode) row to the named workload plane; passing workload_plane_name=null deletes that row. Entries not in the request body are left unchanged.

UpdateTrainingWorkloadPlaneOverrideItemV.description:
One change in a PATCH request body.

``workload_plane_name=None`` deletes the matching ``(gpu_type, is_multinode)``
row; any other value upserts it.

gpu_type.description: GPU type (e.g. 'H100'). null means CPU-only / no-GPU rows.
gpu_type.title: Gpu Type

is_multinode.description: True iff this entry applies to multi-node training (node_count > 1).
is_multinode.title: Is Multinode

workload_plane_name.description: Target workload plane. null deletes the override for (gpu_type, is_multinode); any other value upserts.
workload_plane_name.title: Workload Plane Name

UpdateTrainingWorkloadPlaneOverrideItemV.title: UpdateTrainingWorkloadPlaneOverrideItemV1

overrides.description: Override entries to upsert (workload_plane_name set) or delete (null).
overrides.title: Overrides

name: get_training_job_logs
description: Get the logs for a training job with the provided filters.

GetTrainingJobLogsRequestV1.description: A request to fetch training logs.

start_epoch_millis.description: Epoch milliseconds at which to start fetching logs. Defaults to 30 minutes before the end. The window from start to end must not exceed 7 days.
start_epoch_millis.title: Start Epoch Millis

end_epoch_millis.description: Epoch milliseconds at which to stop fetching logs. Defaults to the current time.
end_epoch_millis.title: End Epoch Millis

limit.description: Limit of logs to fetch in a single request
limit.title: Limit

min_level.description: Minimum log severity to include. Omit to return all log lines, including lines that have no level. Any explicit value returns lines at or above that severity and drops lines without a level.

GetTrainingJobLogsRequestV1.title: GetTrainingJobLogsRequestV1

name: get_training_job_metrics
description: Get the metrics for a training job.

GetTrainingJobMetricsRequestV1.description: A request to fetch metrics. Allows the user to request metrics over a period of time.

end_epoch_millis.description: Epoch millis timestamp to end fetching metrics
end_epoch_millis.title: End Epoch Millis

start_epoch_millis.description: Epoch millis timestamp to start fetching metrics.
start_epoch_millis.title: Start Epoch Millis

step_seconds.description: Resolution of the returned series, in seconds. When omitted, a step is derived from the time range so large windows return fewer points.
step_seconds.title: Step Seconds

GetTrainingJobMetricsRequestV1.title: GetTrainingJobMetricsRequestV1

name: search_training_jobs
description: Search training jobs for the organization.

OrderByV1.description: A request to order training jobs.
OrderByV1.title: OrderByV1

SearchTrainingJobsRequestV1.description: A request to search training jobs.

project_id.description: Filter the training jobs by project ID.
project_id.title: Project Id

job_id.description: Filter the training jobs by job ID.
job_id.title: Job Id

statuses.description: Filter the training jobs by status.
statuses.title: Statuses

order_by.description: Order the training jobs by a field. Currently supports created_at
order_by.title: Order By

SearchTrainingJobsRequestV1.title: SearchTrainingJobsRequestV1

name: list_users
description: Returns the workspace's members. Only actual joined members are returned; service accounts, invited users, and pending join requests are excluded.

cursor.description: Opaque cursor returned by a previous page. Omit to fetch the first page.
cursor.title: Cursor

limit.description: Maximum number of items to return.
limit.title: Limit

name: get_current_user
description: Returns info about the user making the request.

name: get_user
description: Returns info about a user in the caller's workspace.
